data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernization plan:

**Legacy patterns found:**

| # | Pattern | Resolution |
|---|---------|------------|
| 1 | Non-SDK `<Project>` with explicit `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Replace with `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 3 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK auto-globs all `.cs` files |
| 4 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Move metadata to `<PropertyGroup>` properties; delete file |
| 5 | Framework references (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) | Implicit in SDK-style for `net472` |
| 6 | Verbose Debug/Release `PropertyGroup`s (`OutputPath`, `DebugType`, `DebugSymbols`, `ErrorReport`, `WarningLevel`) | All SDK defaults — removable |
| 7 | `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Unnecessary or default in SDK-style |
| 8 | `Configuration`/`Platform` condition defaults | SDK handles these |

Applying now:

<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize them.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Non-SDK-style project format** — uses `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, explicit `Import` statements for `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`
2. **Explicit `Compile` items** — SDK-style auto-globs `.cs` files
3. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK-style projects auto-generate assembly attributes from project properties
4. **Explicit framework references** (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) — implicit in SDK-style


5. **Verbose configuration-specific settings** — SDK defaults handle most of the Debug/Release boilerplate, plus unnecessary properties like `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic`, `ErrorReport`, `WarningLevel`, and `OutputPath` that are either defaults or redundant
6. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** — becomes `TargetFramework: net472`
7. **Debug symbols** — SDK defaults manage `DebugSymbols` and `DebugType` automatically

The modernized version will be drastically simpler. I'll present the analysis of what needs to change, then show the actual SDK-style conversion with explanations for each transformation.

    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 14 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

- **Project root** → `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` (replaces `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, and both `Import` elements)
- **Target framework** → `net472` short moniker (replaces `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2`)
- **Removed** Debug/Release property groups — SDK defaults handle `OutputPath`, `DebugType`, `Optimize`, `DefineConstants`, `WarningLevel`, etc.
- **Removed** explicit `<Compile>` items — SDK auto-globs all `*.cs` files
- **Removed** framework `<Reference>` items (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) — implicit for `net472`
- **Removed** `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `OutputType` (Library is the default)
- **Deleted** `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (`Description`, `Company`, `Product`, `Copyright`, `Version`)
